title: Most Comptroller post-operation verify hooks are disabled or omitted
reason: The missing `*Verify` calls do not create a present exploit in this codebase; harm requires a future or replacement Comptroller that incorrectly assumes these optional post-hooks run, which is too speculative for a reportable protocol bug here.

title: Admin can set reserve factor to 100% and steal all interest
reason: This is intended governance behavior: reserves are protocol-owned by design, and `reserveFactorMaxMantissa` explicitly allows up to 100%.

title: Implementation upgrade can steal all funds
reason: This is the standard explicit trust model of an upgradeable proxy/admin, not an unintended vulnerability in the implementation.

title: Unlimited admin control over interest rate model can cause DoS
reason: Relies on a malicious or compromised admin choosing a bad model; that is governance trust, not a distinct permissionless bug.

title: No timelock on admin actions creates centralization risk
reason: Centralization/governance-risk observation only; no code defect or unintended exploit path is shown.

title: Liquidator can be set to arbitrary address via Comptroller
reason: Liquidation authorization is intentionally delegated to the Comptroller; this does not identify a flaw in the market contract itself.

title: Floating pragma allows different compiler versions
reason: Build-hygiene concern only; no concrete exploit or protocol-level harm is demonstrated from the pragma ranges used here.

title: Integer division precision loss in exchange rate calculation
reason: Normal fixed-point rounding/truncation in Compound-style math; no material loss or exploitable imbalance is shown.

title: sweepToken function vulnerable to ERC-777 reentrancy
reason: `sweepToken` is admin-only, excludes the underlying, and the proposed reentrancy path does not yield a realistic protocol-level exploit.

title: Missing return value check in ERC-20 transfer operations
reason: Contradicted by the code: both `doTransferIn` and `doTransferOut` inspect returndata and revert on `false` via `require(success, ...)`.

title: No panic/pause mechanism for emergencies
reason: Absence of an emergency stop is a governance/design choice, not a concrete vulnerability in the implemented logic.
